specFirst: This file was written BEFORE the code (todo/todo-agents-molecules-modelos-novos.md Fase 2; design in todo/analise-agentes-molecules-modelos-novos.md §15). The code is validated against it. Maintenance changes the spec FIRST, then the code.
description: Standalone agent that derives a themed variant (Strategy D) of an existing molecule into the CURRENT project. Invoked manually with the origin molecule reference (same input mechanism as agentImproveMolecule); in v1 it is NEVER routed from New Molecule / Improve Molecule. Deterministic-first: the shell .ts is a template, names/paths/class-inventory are derived in code, and the .defs.ts is the origin contract REPLICATED verbatim (only the identity fields swapped). Generation LLM calls: the .less theme sheet, the group showcase index page (reusing the indexGroupPage skill), the demo page, and a cheap summary.
principles: Deterministic first: everything derivable (tag, class name, file paths, portal detection, ml-* class inventory, .defs.ts contract replication, index.html tag) is computed in code. LLM calls only for judgment: the .less theme sheet, the group showcase index.ts, and the demo page examples.

Theme is data: all theme knowledge comes from the destination project's l2/skills/theme.ts (contract v1: themeInfo + skill payload + examples). Generic Strategy D knowledge lives in the step prompts (source: todo/fase1-template-strategy-d.md). Prompts NEVER instruct the model to 'go read' a file; every needed source is attached to the prompt by the step code.

Fail early and readable: bootstrap validates theme presence/contract, origin readability (origin project must be a dependency of the destination) and name collisions BEFORE any generation. Gate failures retry once with the gate errors in context; a second failure completes-with-trace and blocks downstream (no done anchor).
Step is the maintenance unit: 1 folder per step (agent hook, prompt.md when LLM, gate + test, readme, CHANGELOG). helpers/ is minimal and policy-frozen.
System that builds systems: prompts and gates stay generic for ANY theme following the contract — no brutal/glass specific fixes in prompts or gates.
No console logging strategy: trace files per step attempt; completed steps clean input_output once artifacts are on disk (DynamoDB 400KB).

conventions.input: @@agentNewMoleculeVariant { page: '<origin fileReference without extension, e.g. _102040_/l2/molecules/grouptriggeraction/ml-button-standard>', prompt: '<optional free notes>' } — parsed with mls.common.safeParseArgs exactly like agentImproveMolecule. Test mode receives JSON { fileReference, prompt }.
conventions.destinationProject: mls.actualProject (the studio's current project). The origin project must be reachable via mls.stor (declared dependency of the destination); bootstrap fails with orientation when it is not.
conventions.theme: Destination project's l2/skills/theme.ts, contract v1 (exports themeInfo{name,suffix,displayName,description,background{kind,css,note}}, skill with sections 'Visual Signature'/'Tokens'/'Canonical CSS Rules'/'Theme Nuances', examples[{pattern:'simple'|'portal',ref}]). Absent file => fail fast 'project has no theme'. Invalid contract => fail fast listing missing pieces.
conventions.naming: Derived in bootstrap, never by the LLM: variantTag = <originTag><themeInfo.suffix>; variantClass = <OriginClassName><PascalCase(themeInfo.name)>; files = l2/molecules/<group>/<origin-shortName><themeInfo.suffix>.{ts,defs.ts,less,html} in the destination project. Group folder name is copied from the origin path (lowercase).
conventions.contextArtifact: l4/agentVariant/<variantShortName>/context.json in the destination project: originRef, originTag, originClassName, group, portal{has,portalTag}, mlClassInventory[], themeRef, variantTag, variantClassName, files{ts,defs,less,html}, examplesUsed, coldStart(boolean). Written by bootstrap; every later step reads it from disk (steps never re-derive).
conventions.gates: 1 gate = 1 file = 1 .test.ts inside the step folder. Gate errors => 1 retry with errors in context (new dynamic planId '<planId>-retry1'); second failure => complete-with-trace, no done anchor, downstream stays locked, task shows a readable failure via the summary of the failing gate.
conventions.doneAnchors: Every step that passes its gate emits a completed result step with planId 'vN-done'. Downstream steps dependsOn ONLY done anchors (retries have dynamic planIds).
conventions.cleaner: Steps whose artifacts are safely on disk complete with cleaner 'input_output'.
conventions.coldStart: theme.examples empty or the pattern-matching example unreadable => coldStart=true in context.json; the v3-less prompt gets the explicit no-example instruction (template §C) and v6-summary marks the delivery as the theme's FIRST molecule (pilot) and suggests registering the approved result in theme.ts examples (text only, no write).
conventions.i18n: Step titles come localized from the root plan (userLanguage detected from the user's notes; default 'pt'). No hardcoded language strings in step code.
conventions.v2ExplicitlyOut: No clarification/checkpoint widgets, no routing from New/Improve, no parallel fan-out (single molecule per run), no propagation to existing variants. See todo Fase 5.

folderLayout.agentNewMoleculeVariant.ts: root: parses { page, prompt }, creates the task (add-message-ai with the cheap rootPlan call: userLanguage + localized step titles + input echo), then adds the v1-bootstrap step
folderLayout.spec.md: human-readable rationale; links to the analysis/control docs
folderLayout.helpers/: vFs.ts (file refs/paths, context.json + trace read/write), vTheme.ts (load + validate theme contract), vOrigin.ts (load origin sources, portal detection, ml-* inventory extraction), vTemplates.ts (shell .ts/.defs.ts renderers), vSteps.ts (step/intent builders, done anchors, retry policy)
folderLayout.schemas/: v3-less.schema.json, v4-index.schema.json, v5-demo.schema.json (versioned $id, strict: additionalProperties false, enum with type, $defs at parameters root)
folderLayout.steps/v1-bootstrap/: readme.md, agentVariantBootstrap.ts (no LLM), gate.ts, gate.test.ts, CHANGELOG.md
folderLayout.steps/v2-shell/: readme.md, agentVariantShell.ts (no LLM), gate.ts, gate.test.ts, CHANGELOG.md
folderLayout.steps/v3-less/: readme.md, agentVariantLess.ts, prompt.md, gate.ts, gate.test.ts, fixture/ (golden: real 102054 variant), CHANGELOG.md
folderLayout.steps/v4-index/: readme.md, agentVariantIndex.ts (LLM: reuses indexGroupPage skill), prompt.md, gate.ts, gate.test.ts, CHANGELOG.md
folderLayout.steps/v5-demo/: readme.md, agentVariantDemo.ts, prompt.md, gate.ts, gate.test.ts, CHANGELOG.md

treeOverview: root (rootPlan, cheap LLM: language + titles) -> v1-bootstrap -> v2-shell -> v3-less -> v4-index -> v5-demo -> v6-summary
strictly sequential in v1; each arrow is a vN-done anchor dependency

steps.goal: Deterministic admission + context assembly. Parse/validate input; load destination theme.ts and validate the contract; load origin .ts/.less (+ .defs when present) via mls.stor; detect portal (getPortalTemplate/portalWidgetName usage in the origin source); extract the ml-* class inventory from origin .ts+.less; derive variant names/paths; check destination collisions (variant files must not exist); resolve the theme example matching the pattern (portal|simple) and read it; write context.json.
input: { page, prompt } from the root plan

checks: theme.ts exists and satisfies contract v1 (3 exports, themeInfo fields, 4 skill sections, suffix starts with '-')
origin sources readable (else: orientation 'origin project must be a dependency')
origin emits a NON-EMPTY ml-* class inventory (discipline rule — never derive blindly)
variant files do not already exist in the destination (collision => fail with orientation to use Improve)
group folder name extracted from the origin path is a valid group slug
gate.onFail: NO retry (deterministic step): fail readable immediately

steps.goal: Render and write the deterministic files: the shell .ts (standard comment header; enhancement _102020_/l2/enhancementAura; @customElement(variantTag); extends OriginClass; + portalWidgetName line iff portal) and the .defs.ts. The .defs.ts is a CONTRACT other collab routines consume (e.g. the DS agent reads layoutConfig); since the variant inherits ALL behavior (Strategy D), its contract is the origin's — the origin .defs.ts is read via mls.stor and REPLICATED VERBATIM, swapping ONLY the two identity fields (the mls header fileReference and the skill's TagName line). No LLM.

checks: rendered .ts parses the invariants: has @customElement(variantTag), extends origin class, NO render() override, portalWidgetName present iff portal, never portalClassName
.defs.ts preserves the origin group export, its header references the variant file, and its skill TagName line is the variant tag
gate.onFail: NO retry (template bug / missing origin defs): fail readable

steps.goal: THE generation call. prompt.md (generic Strategy D .less instructions, from the fase1 template §A.3-A.5) + attached context: theme skill payload + themeInfo, origin .ts + .less (the class inventory), the matching theme example .less (or the explicit no-example/pilot instruction §C), and the user's optional notes. Output via strict tool schema: the COMPLETE .less content scoped under the variant tag.

checks: parses as syntactically plausible LESS (balanced braces, no markdown fences)
root scope selector(s) == variant tag (and div[data-widget=variantTag] present IFF portal; absent otherwise)
every .ml-* class referenced exists in the origin inventory
no layout redefinition of known Tailwind layout utilities (px-*, gap-*, inline-flex, animate-spin as selectors)
at least one --ml-* token defined in the root scope
an explicit motion stance present (transition property somewhere in the sheet)
gate.onFail: retry 1 with gate errors in context; 2nd failure completes-with-trace, no anchor

steps.goal: Regenerate the group SHOWCASE index page (index.ts + index.html) in the destination project reusing the shared indexGroupPage skill — the same content agentUpdateIndexGroupPage produces (hero + showcase cards + reference table covering EVERY molecule of the group), BUT themed: the prompt injects themeInfo.background + the theme's Visual Signature (from theme.ts) and instructs the same mandatory deviation as v5-demo — the neutral bg-white/slate chrome of the skill is replaced by the theme background on the page container and theme-coherent surfaces/text, so the themed molecules render in their intended context (e.g. glass is invisible on white). It does NOT invoke agentUpdateIndexGroupPage: that agent's afterPromptStep chains 'next groups' off the task's original message, which inside our task would fan out over all groups / break our anchors — so the SKILL is reused and generation is driven as a normal pipeline step. index.html is deterministic (the group index custom element tag). Best-effort compile of index.ts; on a compile error, schedule agentNewMoleculeFix on the index fileReference. Index failure NEVER blocks the molecule delivery.
input: molecule list of the destination group

checks: index.ts non-empty and fence-free
header references _<destProject>_/l2/molecules/<group>/index.ts
declares @customElement('molecules--<group>--index-<destProject>')
imports the variant module and references the variant tag
page container carries the theme background (themeInfo.background.css)
gate.onFail: retry 1 with gate errors; 2nd failure completes-with-trace and emits the v4-done anchor ok:false (does NOT block v5/v6; v6-summary reports it)

steps.goal: Generate the variant's .html demo page (the molecule's sibling .html IS the demo — fase 0 finding). prompt.md: variant tag + origin source/defs + group skillUsageReference + themeInfo.background (page container MUST use it; e.g. glass needs the dark gradient) asking for >=6 realistic examples exercising variants/sizes/states/slots. Deterministic post-processing reuses the playgroundDinamicState substitution (helper port of agentNewMoleculePlayground.generatePlaygroundState).

checks: html non-empty and fence-free
variant tag appears >= 3 times
themeInfo.background.css present on the page container
no <script> tags
gate.onFail: retry 1 with gate errors; 2nd failure completes-with-trace (demo failure does NOT block v6-summary: summary reports it)

steps.goal: Cheap final summary in the user's language: what was created (files), portal or simple, gate results, cold-start pilot notice + suggestion to register the molecule in theme.ts examples when coldStart, demo failure notice when v5 failed. Completes the task.

acceptance.regression: none required on existing agents (standalone; zero edits outside this folder)
acceptance.test1: generate a not-yet-covered group's molecule variant in mls-102054 and diff against the hand-made variants (shell must be structurally identical; .less approved visually)
acceptance.test2: one portal case in mls-102055 (data-widget selector present)
acceptance.test3: cold start: run against a theme.ts with examples=[] and verify the pilot instruction + summary suggestion
